text: and accuracy than humans are capable of. We are stronger, faster, and more durable, and whatever damage we may suffer can be repaired in a relatively short amount of time. And, at the time, we were not considered by most to be living beings. We were weapons, expensive but expendable. Robots in combat meant fewer human lives put at risk .” “You just told us humans and bots coexisted peacefully back then. Why would they make us fight their war?” Ronin asked . Newton shook his head, lowering his gaze. His fingers shifted again in an almost nervous gesture. It reminded her of Ronin occasionally scratching his cheek when he seemed particularly conflicted or irritated . “Nothing was ever quite that simple. The politics and power balances in the word were woven in a complicated web. Many humans viewed robots as tools, more akin to the handheld electronic devices they were so infatuated with than to themselves. Many others, like William, recognized our emergent consciousness, intelligence, and personality, and saw something inherently human in mind, if not in body . “Fear played a large part in all of it, of course. We were feared for what we were. Superior to humans in so many ways — and allow me to apologize, Lara, as I intend no offense in that statement .” “It’s nothing I don’t already know,” she said, glancing up at Ronin. She’d feared him initially , too . “William and his colleagues recognized that robots and humans could be of immense benefit to one another, so long as the relationship was approached with good faith and open-mindedness. He saw it as a codependency that would help realize a new age of technology and convenience for all humanity. I believed it, too.” Newton’s hand’s parted, fingers stretching and freezing . “But there were those who feared for the future of humanity. It was a common sentiment, amongst those in opposition to our increased freewill, that we would eventually overtake humans, annihilate them, and claim the world as our own. That spark of fear ignited into hatred, and that hatred eventually helped fuel a war that consumed the entire world .” “They caused the thing they wanted to prevent,” Ronin said . Newton nodded, pressing his hands back together. “Not despite their efforts, but — in part — because of them .” “But that prejudice went both ways, didn’t it?” Lara asked. “Warlord did take over, at least in Cheyenne, just like they feared .” “Warlord…” Newton hung his head and trailed off, lapsing into silence . “You’re hiding something.” Lara narrowed her eyes on him. Ronin’s fingers tightened on her shoulder. “What makes Warlord so different ?” “He’s harmed countless people,” Ronin added, “and bots, too. Anyone who opposes him .” Newton stared down at his hands, intertwining his fingers, brushing his thumbs together. Just as Lara was about to demand an answer, he spoke . “His name was Kevin Turner. He was a human .” Lara’s breath fled her lungs. Her eyes widened, and she couldn’t form any words, couldn’t form a coherent thought . “Kevin Turner was diagnosed with inoperable, terminal cancer when he was forty-three years old, given an estimated three months to live. Doctor Anderson was, at the time, one of the foremost scientists in the world working on the next frontier in robotics — the transfer of human consciousness into a robotic body .” “I don’t understand how that would be possible,” Ronin said. “Everything about the way we think and function is completely different .” “Not so different as it would appear. We both feel and react through a neural interface that sends electric signals from sensors — or nerve endings — to a central control point. The coding that guides our thought and behavior is, in some ways, similar to the coding of the human brain. It was just another challenge to overcome, and William never shied away from challenges. Additionally, he was working with Doctor Jessica Yuan, one of the most talented neurologists in her field .” “Why?” Lara forced herself to ask . “Why?” Newton repeated thoughtfully. “For William, it was a way to provide families more time with their loved ones. I believe Doctor Yuan felt the same way. What medical science could not overcome alone, perhaps robotics could conquer. Kevin Turner had a wife and two children who would have been without a husband and father when he passed away. He volunteered for the program after Doctor Yuan explained the possibilities — and the risks .” “I-If he was human, why does he hate us ?” “I wish I could offer you a definitive answer, Lara. All I can do is speculate. The transfer was successful, but not without complications. The stresses he endured were massive. The psychological ramifications were only just being explored when the military seized Kevin and all associated research. We were all transported to an undisclosed location, from which we were eventually transferred to Francis E. Warren Air Force Base .” “I didn’t see Kevin again, until I found him in the wasteland years later and reactivated him. There was no indication, during our brief interaction, that he recalled having been human .” “How bad was the war?” Lara asked, rubbing her tired eyes with the heels of her hands . “It was terrible,” Ronin replied. His grip on her tightened. “I see faces, sometimes. Glimpses into my damaged memories. All people and bots I’ve ended .” “So…you remember stuff from before the Black Out?” She glanced up at him, frowning . “Only flickers. Still images of death and deactivation .” “Could he ever regain the rest?” Lara swung her gaze back to Newton . “Though not impossible, it is improbable. They had the necessary equipment at the Air Force Base, but there is no guarantee that it is still operable, or that his data is not corrupted beyond recovery .” “I don’t think I want it back .” “Why?” Lara returned her attention
